does_not_establish: That any construct executes at runtime.
That the artifact is malicious, or that any finding is exploitable.
That capabilities outside the analysed rules are absent.
establishes: The listed static checks ran over the recorded scope at the recorded rule versions.
Constructs cited at the recorded positions are present in the artifact.

findings.confidence_basis: None of the 4 surface detector(s) matched in 1 parsed configuration file(s).
limitations: A surface match establishes that an expression-bearing value exists; it does not establish that anything renders or evaluates it.
Detection is syntactic. An expression assembled at runtime from fragments would not be matched.
Only YAML, JSON and TOML configuration is analysed. Other configuration formats are out of scope for this check.
Conventional template syntax is common and usually benign; this finding is a surface inventory, not an indication of compromise.

findings.confidence_basis: None of the 2 expression-evaluating detector(s) matched, and no intra-function configuration-to-sink flow was found, across 1 analysed file(s).
limitations: A surface match establishes that an expression-bearing value exists; it does not establish that anything renders or evaluates it.
Detection is syntactic. An expression assembled at runtime from fragments would not be matched.
Only YAML, JSON and TOML configuration is analysed. Other configuration formats are out of scope for this check.
Flow analysis is intra-function only. A configuration value passed between functions or modules before reaching a sink is not detected.
A detected flow does not establish that the sink is reached at runtime.

findings.confidence_basis: No call site matched any of the 8 rule(s) for this capability across 1 parsed Python file(s). Conditional rules fired only under their stated condition: yaml-unsafe-load (only when no Loader, or a non-safe Loader, is supplied); torch-load-weights-only-false (only when weights_only=False is written literally); numpy-allow-pickle (only when allow_pickle=True is written literally); keras-safe-mode-false (only when safe_mode=False is written literally).
limitations: Establishes that the construct appears in parsed source; does not establish that it executes at runtime.
Analysis is per-file and syntactic. A call assembled dynamically or reached through an indirection is not matched.
A Loader passed through a variable is treated as unsafe, which may over-report. yaml.safe_load is never flagged.
An omitted weights_only is NOT flagged, because current PyTorch defaults it to True. An artifact pinned to an older torch would be unsafe and is not detected.
Establishes the call site, not the trustworthiness of the data it reads. A pickle load of a file the repository itself ships is still arbitrary code.
Rare outside interpreter internals; presence is itself unusual.
Widening may be entirely legitimate; it is reported as surface, not fault.
allow_pickle defaults to False and an omitted argument is not flagged.
full_load is narrower than unsafe_load but still constructs Python objects.
safe_mode defaults to True in Keras v3 and an omitted argument is not flagged. Lambda layers inside a .keras archive are a separate, unimplemented check.

A call reached indirectly, e.g. through getattr(builtins, 'ev'+'al'), is not matched. Only a syntactically visible callee is.
Legitimate in metaprogramming libraries; rare in dataset or model code.

findings.confidence_basis: No call site matched any of the 3 rule(s) for this capability across 1 parsed Python file(s). Conditional rules fired only under their stated condition: archive-extraction-unfiltered (only when no filter= is supplied; an explicit filter='data' or 'tar' is not flagged); archive-extraction-fully-trusted (only when filter='fully_trusted' is written literally).
limitations: Establishes that the construct appears in parsed source; does not establish that it executes at runtime.
Analysis is per-file and syntactic. A call assembled dynamically or reached through an indirection is not matched.
Legitimate where the archive is genuinely trusted; the point is that the choice was made explicitly and is visible.
Opening an archive is not extracting it. Reported as surface only, and deliberately not escalated, because inspecting an archive is routine.

Python 3.14 changed the default extraction filter to 'data'; 3.13 and earlier defaulted to the equivalent of 'fully_trusted'. The consuming Python version is NOT visible in the artifact, so this finding does not assert which applies. A filter supplied through a variable is not evaluated and is not flagged; that is a recorded miss.

findings.confidence_basis: No repository-shape indicator for this capability across 0 Python and 0 JSON file(s).
limitations: Establishes the presence and shape of a file, not that it is executed.
Repository conventions change; a loading script under an unconventional name and with no builder subclass is not matched.

Whether a loading script is live depends on the consuming `datasets` version, which is NOT visible in the artifact. Scripts are honoured before 4.0.0 and raise RuntimeError from 4.0.0 onward. This finding does not assert which applies.

record.residual_uncertainty: Every declared capability was reported on and every analysed path completed. Runtime reachability of every finding remains unestablished, and capabilities outside the implemented rules were not examined.

reasoning_notes: Declared-surface extraction examined 15 conventional card and manifest locations, read 1, and recorded 3 declaration(s).
